mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate transmits easier financial conditions into equity valuations through a lower discount rate and a weaker hurdle for risk assets, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the policy path is clearly downward.
failure_condition: Reject if 20-day forward SPX returns are not positive in a majority of instances during falling-FEDFUNDS periods, or if walk-forward Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in UNRATE signals weakening household income and demand, which compresses corporate revenue expectations and pushes S&P 500 returns negative over the next 20 trading days after the unemployment rate crosses a deterioration threshold.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if triggered UNRATE deterioration episodes are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns more than 60% of the time, or if the signal's rolling 2-year Rank IC is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: A synchronized acceleration in GDPC1 and RSAFS indicates broad end-demand strength, lifting revenue expectations and supporting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because earnings revisions follow aggregate spending momentum.
failure_condition: Reject if joint GDPC1-RSAFS strength does not outperform either series alone in predicting positive 60-day SPX returns, or if the interaction term has no incremental explanatory power over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in INDPRO after recent strength signals an industrial slowdown and margin pressure from weaker production utilization, which should translate into negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO declines for 2 consecutive monthly observations
failure_condition: Reject if consecutive INDPRO declines are followed by positive 20-day SPX returns in more than half of cases, or if the post-trigger average return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: When HOUST falls while UNRATE rises, the housing and labor channels jointly signal household balance-sheet stress and weaker consumption, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if the HOUST-down/UNRATE-up combination does not produce lower 60-day SPX returns than either signal alone, or if the interaction coefficient is not stable across rolling samples.

mechanism_narrative: A FEDFUNDS rate above 4.5% in a tightening cycle raises discount rates and compresses equity multiples, making S&P 500 returns negative over the next 5 trading days once the policy rate clears that threshold.
failure_condition: Reject if high-FEDFUNDS episodes are not followed by negative 5-day SPX returns more often than not, or if the signal loses significance after controlling for recent CPIAUCSL changes.

mechanism_narrative: In a post-shock recovery, falling UNRATE combined with rising PAYEMS signals labor-market reacceleration, supporting higher consumption and positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if joint labor improvement does not improve prediction relative to standalone labor series, or if positive 20-day SPX returns fail to dominate when UNRATE falls and PAYEMS rises together.

mechanism_narrative: A deceleration in CPIAUCSL below the recent trend while RSAFS remains firm lowers real-rate pressure and preserves nominal demand, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days in disinflationary expansion regimes.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL inflation trend slows for 3 consecutive months
failure_condition: Reject if disinflation plus steady sales does not outperform either input alone for 60-day SPX returns, or if the combined signal is not positive in at least 55% of triggered instances.
